meta.project: Learning Management System (LMS) for Schools & Colleges

product_overview: A role-based LMS for schools and colleges covering academic core workflows, enrollment, attendance, reporting, and administrative control. The system supports Admin, Teacher, and Student access with scoped permissions for courses, applications, enrollments, and institutional analytics.
core_goals: Support role-based access for Admin, Teacher, and Student users
Enable authentication and secure token-based session management
Provide course, enrollment, and application management workflows
Allow institutional reporting and overview analytics
Support user account administration for school/college operations

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Authenticate users, manage access tokens, and return the current user profile.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with access token, refresh token, and session data -> GET /api/v1/auth/me with access token -> Receive 200 with current user profile
POST /api/v1/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with valid auth token -> Receive 200 with new access token
POST /api/v1/auth/refresh without a valid token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
POST /api/v1/auth/logout with valid auth token -> Receive 200 and session invalidation
GET /api/v1/auth/me without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error

features.name: Users API
features.description: Admin-only user management for creating and retrieving Teacher and Student accounts.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/users with valid authenticated Admin request and user payload -> Receive 201 with created user record -> GET /api/v1/users/:id with auth token -> Receive 200 with the created user details
GET /api/v1/users with authenticated Admin request -> Receive 200 with list of all users
POST /api/v1/users with missing required fields -> Receive 400 validation error
GET /api/v1/users/:id with nonexistent user ID -> Receive 404 not found error
GET /api/v1/users without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error

features.name: Courses API
features.description: Course management for creating and listing courses within the institution.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/courses with valid authenticated request and course payload -> Receive 201 with created course record -> GET /api/v1/courses with auth token -> Receive 200 with course list including the new course
GET /api/v1/courses with authenticated request -> Receive 200 with all available courses
POST /api/v1/courses with missing or invalid course data -> Receive 400 validation error
POST /api/v1/courses without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
GET /api/v1/courses with auth token but no matching courses -> Receive 200 with an empty list

features.name: Enrollments API
features.description: Student enrollment, course applications, and application review workflows.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/applications with valid public application data -> Receive 201 with application status pending review -> GET /api/v1/enrollments with authenticated Admin request -> Receive 200 with enrollment records after approval or direct enrollment
POST /api/v1/applications with incomplete application payload -> Receive 400 validation error

PATCH /api/v1/applications/:id/review with authenticated Admin request and approve decision -> Receive 200 with reviewed application status -> POST /api/v1/enrollments with student and course data -> Receive 201 with created direct enrollment record
PATCH /api/v1/applications/:id/review without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
PATCH /api/v1/applications/:id/review with invalid application ID -> Receive 404 not found error
POST /api/v1/enrollments with invalid student or course references -> Receive 400 validation or 404 not found error
GET /api/v1/enrollments with authenticated Admin request -> Receive 200 with all enrollments

features.name: Reports API
features.description: Institution overview and risk analytics for administrators.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/reports/overview with authenticated Admin request -> Receive 200 with institution overview metrics
GET /api/v1/reports/at-risk with authenticated Admin request -> Receive 200 with at-risk student list and associated indicators
GET /api/v1/reports/overview without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
GET /api/v1/reports/at-risk without auth token -> Receive 401 unauthorized error
GET /api/v1/reports/at-risk with authenticated request but no qualifying records -> Receive 200 with an empty result set
